title: Permission Policy Review, Root-Group Alignment, And Regression Validation Tasklist
mainGoal: Review the permission policy used by installer, setup, and the `tenant_create` CLI, change the created-folder policy so created paths use the `root` group and stay broadly readable and traversable even when `root` is not the owner, keep file writes controlled by ownership while giving the `root` group the intended read and write authority where policy requires it, then rerun the previously failing lifecycle checks to confirm the detected problems have actually been corrected.

tasklistPolicy.orderingRule: Ordered by dependency chain: first define the permission contract precisely, then apply it consistently in policy and scripts, then validate the tenant-access path, and finally rerun the known failing lifecycle checks plus the cleanup regressions.
tasklistPolicy.focusRule: This tasklist is intentionally narrower than the broader installer lifecycle tasklists. It focuses on filesystem ownership, group assignment, mode and ACL policy, and direct proof that the recent smoke-test failures were corrected by those changes.
tasklistPolicy.readerNote: The current reports show that the scaffold tenant host config is still unreadable to the manager runtime user during live boot. This tasklist turns that operational failure into a permission-policy review plus one mandatory regression validation pass.

sourceReports.topic: Latest installer, loopback smoke, uninstall, purge, and cleanup follow-up run
Earlier lifecycle run showing the same manager ACL blocker and cleanup residue
Current shared user, tenant layout, and ACL policy defaults
Current installer setup path for runtime directories, ownership, metadata, and first-tenant scaffold
Current tenant scaffold creation, ownership, mode, and role ACL behavior

taskGroups.title: Define The Target Permission Contract
taskGroups.goal: Turn the requested root-group ownership direction into one explicit contract for owners, groups, modes, and ACL behavior before implementation starts.
pointsOfAttention: The request combines two potentially conflicting ideas: `only the owner can write files` and `root group can read write`.

The implementation phase should not guess its way through that conflict; this group should define which paths need owner-only writes, which paths allow group writes, and whether those rules differ for files versus directories.
The result should be one concrete permission matrix that installer, setup, tenant creation, runtime users, and cleanup scripts can all follow.

tasks.title: Write the desired ownership and mode matrix
tasks.reportedGoal: Define exactly who owns each directory tier, which group each path uses, and which read, write, and traverse permissions each actor should have.

subtasks.title: List the required rules for `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, `/srv/opt/ehecoatl`, tenant domain folders, tenant host folders, and scaffolded files such as `config.json`

Resolve the requested write-policy ambiguity explicitly: decide whether the `root` group should truly retain write access on files, or whether file writes remain owner-only while `root` group access is limited to read and traverse on selected paths
Document whether the same policy applies to files and directories, or whether directories intentionally allow broader group write while files stay stricter

tasks.title: Define the runtime-access strategy around the new root-group policy
tasks.reportedGoal: Ensure the manager, engine, tenant users, and root-operated maintenance flows still work once group ownership changes.

subtasks.title: Decide whether runtime access should come mainly from Unix group ownership, ACLs, or a hybrid of both
Define whether `e_manager` and `e_engine` should rely on membership in `root`, explicit ACL entries, or both
Confirm how the host-derived tenant users should interact with the `root` group without weakening tenant isolation unnecessarily

taskGroups.title: Align Runtime Policy Defaults With The New Contract
taskGroups.goal: Move the declared policy in `runtime-policy.json` to the new ownership and group model so the scripts stop encoding a different filesystem contract than the one the project intends to enforce.
pointsOfAttention: Do not leave the JSON policy saying `ehecoatl` while the scripts implement `root`, or vice versa.
The tenant layout defaults should become the single declarative source for domain and host ownership and mode expectations.
Any group-mode change for tenant users should be reviewed against the process-user naming and isolation model, not only the immediate smoke failure.

tasks.title: Update system and tenant layout defaults
tasks.reportedGoal: Make the declared owner, group, and mode defaults reflect the new root-group contract.

subtasks.title: Review `system.sharedGroup`, `tenantLayout.domainBaseGroup`, `tenantLayout.hostGroup`, and any related mode values for root-group alignment
Update the policy defaults so newly created runtime and tenant paths inherit the intended `root` group behavior
Confirm the resulting policy still supports the intended tenant-user owner model without depending on accidental ACL side effects

tasks.title: Review tenant access declarations under the new model
tasks.reportedGoal: Ensure the manager and engine access rules stay coherent when group ownership changes.

subtasks.title: Re-evaluate whether the current `tenantAccess.manager` and `tenantAccess.engine` ACL lists remain necessary, or whether some of that access should become native group access
Keep explicit ACLs only where the new root-group policy does not already guarantee the needed access
Make sure the manager can read every scaffold host config path needed for tenant scanning and route matching

taskGroups.title: Implement The New Ownership Rules In Installer And Setup
taskGroups.goal: Apply the agreed permission contract to the runtime directories and install metadata created by the installer and setup flow.
pointsOfAttention: The setup script currently creates the standard directories first and then recursively assigns `ehecoatl:ehecoatl` ownership with `750` permissions.
The installation metadata path under `/etc/opt/ehecoatl` should be included in the same review, because uninstall currently claims to remove it but leaves it behind.
Global readability and traversal should be applied deliberately; avoid using recursive modes that accidentally over-broaden sensitive writable paths.

tasks.title: Rework standard runtime directory ownership and modes
tasks.reportedGoal: Make setup create `/etc`, `/var`, and `/srv` trees with the intended owner, `root` group, and access bits.

subtasks.title: Replace the current recursive `chown -R "$EHECOATL_USER:$EHECOATL_GROUP"` and blanket `chmod -R 750` pattern with path-specific ownership and mode assignment
Ensure the resulting directory permissions still let the active runtime users boot, scan tenants, write to their operational folders, and preserve the intended security boundary
Review whether setgid should be applied on selected directories so newly created children keep the intended `root` group automatically

tasks.title: Correct install metadata ownership and uninstall cleanup
tasks.reportedGoal: Make metadata readable and removable under the new policy without repeating the currently observed residue bug.

subtasks.title: Set explicit owner, group, and mode rules for `/etc/opt/ehecoatl/install-meta.env` after it is written
Verify `setup/uninstall.sh` really removes `install-meta.env` instead of only logging success
Keep the documented preservation boundary for `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, and `/srv/opt/ehecoatl` coherent after these changes

taskGroups.title: Implement The New Ownership Rules In Tenant Creation
taskGroups.goal: Make `tenant_create` produce host and domain trees that follow the new root-group contract and no longer block manager route scanning.
pointsOfAttention: The latest failure reports point directly at `www.<domain>/config.json` readability for the manager process.
Recursive `chown -R "$TENANT_USER:$TENANT_GROUP"` and `chmod -R "$HOST_MODE"` are likely too blunt for the intended mixed-access model.
If group ownership becomes `root`, the CLI should preserve tenant ownership while preventing unintended write exposure.

tasks.title: Refactor tenant domain and host ownership creation
tasks.reportedGoal: Create domain and host paths with the intended owner and `root` group instead of the current host-user group model.

subtasks.title: Review how `TENANT_GROUP`, `DEFAULT_GROUP`, `tenantLayout.hostGroup`, and `tenantLayout.domainBaseGroup` interact today
Update creation and post-copy ownership steps so tenant paths keep the intended owner while shifting to the `root` group model
Set path-specific modes for directories and files rather than applying one recursive host mode everywhere

tasks.title: Rework ACL and traversal handling around the new group model
tasks.reportedGoal: Avoid relying on missing or incomplete ACL entries for essential manager access.

subtasks.title: Review whether `grant_tenant_runtime_traversal` and `apply_role_acl` still need to grant manager read access to `config.json` when group ownership is corrected
Keep explicit ACLs only for cases that the new owner/group/mode policy still does not cover
Verify that a freshly scaffolded `www.fakedomain.com` host gives manager route scanning access without depending on ad hoc manual repairs

taskGroups.title: Run Focused Permission Regression Checks
taskGroups.goal: Prove on disk that the new permission contract is actually being applied to all created paths after installer, setup, and tenant creation run.
pointsOfAttention: Do not rely only on code inspection here; the filesystem state after a real run matters.
This group should verify owner, group, mode bits, setgid bits if used, and any remaining ACL entries.
The checks should cover both runtime base directories and a freshly scaffolded tenant host tree.

tasks.title: Inspect created path ownership and modes after setup
tasks.reportedGoal: Verify the base runtime tree matches the intended root-group permission policy.

subtasks.title: Run a fresh setup or installer flow and inspect `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, `/srv/opt/ehecoatl`, and `install-meta.env` with `ls -ld` and `getfacl`
Confirm each inspected path matches the declared owner, `root` group, and mode rules from the new contract
Record any path that still inherits the old `ehecoatl` or tenant-user group unexpectedly

tasks.title: Inspect scaffolded tenant ownership and modes after `tenant_create`
tasks.reportedGoal: Verify the tenant tree no longer reproduces the failing manager-access pattern.

subtasks.title: Scaffold a fresh `www.fakedomain.com` tenant and inspect the domain directory, host directory, and host `config.json`
Confirm those paths now use the intended owner, `root` group, and path-specific read, write, and traverse permissions
Confirm any remaining ACLs are intentional and not compensating for a broken base group policy

taskGroups.title: Rerun The Previously Failing Lifecycle Checks
taskGroups.goal: Confirm the permission-policy changes actually fix the concrete runtime and cleanup problems detected in the recent installer lifecycle reports.
pointsOfAttention: This is the proof group; the permission-policy work is not complete until the observed failures are rechecked.
Use the same end-to-end shape as the latest lifecycle report so the comparison is trustworthy.
If a failure remains, the rerun report should name the exact remaining blocker rather than claiming partial improvement without evidence.

tasks.title: Rerun the installer and loopback smoke lifecycle
tasks.reportedGoal: Verify the manager can now read the scaffold host config and serve the expected smoke routes.

subtasks.title: Repeat the disposable installer clone flow with `fakedomain.com` as the scaffold domain
Start the runtime and verify `GET /`, `GET /htm/index.htm`, `GET /htm/cached.htm`, `GET /hello`, and `GET /session` using `Host: fakedomain.com` or `Host: www.fakedomain.com`
Confirm PM2 logs no longer show manager crash-loop `EACCES` against `/var/opt/ehecoatl/tenants/<domain>/www/config.json`

tasks.title: Rerun uninstall and cleanup residue checks
tasks.reportedGoal: Verify the recent cleanup regressions are corrected under the updated permission model.

subtasks.title: Confirm uninstall removes `install-meta.env` for real instead of leaving it behind until purge
Confirm purge leaves no stopped root PM2 `Ehecoatl` entry, or explicitly implement and verify the intended cleanup behavior
Write a new follow-up performance report that compares the new run against the March 23, 2026 failing runs and states whether the detected problems are now corrected

executionGateForPermissionPolicyReview.description: This permission-policy effort is complete only when the installer, setup flow, and `tenant_create` CLI all create paths according to one explicit root-group permission contract, a fresh scaffold tenant no longer blocks manager route scanning with `EACCES`, uninstall removes installation metadata correctly, purge leaves no PM2 residue, and one new follow-up performance report records the corrected outcome.
requiredChecks: The owner, group, mode, and ACL contract for runtime and tenant paths is written explicitly and the write-policy ambiguity is resolved
Runtime-policy defaults reflect the new `root` group ownership model consistently
Installer and setup create `/etc/opt/ehecoatl`, `/var/opt/ehecoatl`, `/srv/opt/ehecoatl`, and installation metadata with the intended ownership and permissions
`tenant_create` creates domain and host paths with the intended owner, `root` group, and path-specific permissions
A fresh `www.fakedomain.com` scaffold no longer reproduces manager `EACCES` for host `config.json`
The loopback smoke routes succeed for `/`, `/htm/index.htm`, `/htm/cached.htm`, `/hello`, and `/session`
Uninstall removes `install-meta.env` correctly and purge leaves no residual PM2 `Ehecoatl` entry
A new follow-up performance report records whether the previously detected problems were corrected
